_README: EXAMPLE per-work-folder config overlay. Copy this to  <WorkDir>/verify_config.json  and edit.
It is deep-merged over VerifyConfig.psd1 at startup. Precedence: CLI args > this JSON > .psd1 > session fallback.
Only include the keys you want to override - every other setting falls back to VerifyConfig.psd1.
Tip: run  VerifyTool.ps1 -Phase InitConfig  inside your WorkDir. First run generates a FULL file;
when the file already exists it REPAIRS instead: your settings are kept as-is and ONLY fields the
tool gained since the file's _SCHEMA stamp are appended (a sparse file stays sparse; leave _SCHEMA
alone -- it is ignored at runtime). Use the f=Force option to regenerate the full snapshot.
Run  VerifyTool.ps1 -Phase InitConfig -Interactive  then pick w to walk a group field-by-field
(Enter=keep, value=set, -d=delete, q=stop; no path typing needed), or v/e/d by JSON path; s to save.
J4EvidenceDir and Address below are the SINGLE canonical fields (they replaced the duplicated
DeliverFiles.J4EvidenceDir / Mail.EvidenceFolder / Reviewer.Address, which still win if an old file sets them).
InitConfig also writes verify_config.README.txt with field explanations; JSON itself cannot use // comments.
Save as UTF-8 (no BOM). Japanese text is fine as plain UTF-8.

Window._CropByFolderExample: CropLeft/CropTop/CropRight/CropBottom above are per-side overrides applied globally to every HM/MQ/Jenkins snapshot (-1 = inherit CropPx for that side). CropByFolder narrows this further to one snap folder (GIFT_HM/GFIX_HM/GIFT_MQ/GIFT_Jenkins/GFIX_Jenkins/GIFT_noGfixfile): each entry may set any of Left/Top/Right/Bottom (px), and a side left out falls back to the resolved global Crop<Side>/CropPx value. Delete the two example CropByFolder entries above if you don't need per-folder overrides -- an empty CropByFolder ({}) is the shipped default and changes nothing.

Mark._TemplateExample: Add "Template": "gift_hm_status.png" (+ optional Tolerance/PadX/PadY/PadWidth/PadHeight) to a Boxes entry to try image-recognition placement first, falling back to OffsetX/OffsetY/Width/Height above when no match is found. If the entry ALSO has its own Width/Height (like GIFT_Jenkins/GFIX_Jenkins above), Template only supplies the anchor point and that configured size is kept as-is (PadX/PadY shift the anchor; PadWidth/PadHeight add a constant margin to Width/Height); without Width/Height the box is sized to the matched crop itself (padded by PadX/PadY per side, plus PadWidth/PadHeight). PadWidth/PadHeight are a fixed number for every correl in the folder -- they do not auto-track a varying on-page content length (e.g. Jenkins filenames of different lengths); that needs a measured per-correl source, not yet wired here. See mark_templates/README.txt.
Mark._StampImageExample: Add "StampImage": "already_exists.png" alongside "Template" on a Boxes entry to insert that image (native size) at the Template match location instead of a rectangle. No match = no stamp, no fixed-offset fallback -- used for GIFT_noGfixfile above so already_exists.png only appears when NoGfixHit.png is actually found. See mark_templates/README.txt.
Mark._RowHeightExample: GIFT_MQ's OffsetY (176.9) is calibrated for BaseRow=2 (the common 2-record case). Some correls show 1, 3, or more MQ records, and the box must always land on the LAST/newest one. RowHeight (points, >0) has Mark.ps1 add (actualRow - BaseRow) * RowHeight to OffsetY; actualRow/recordCount come from snap\GIFT_MQ\<correl>.mqrow.json (written at snap time), else a re-parse of <correl>.txt, else English OCR of <correl>.png. RowHeight=63.8 was measured from a real J4 evidence workbook (Probe-Shapes.ps1); RowHeight=0 disables the shift and keeps the legacy fixed offset if it needs to be turned back off.
